response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates exceptional software engineering fundamentals and a proven track record of building scalable, well-tested systems, there is a critical misalignment with this role's core requirements. Most significantly, C++ expertise ended in 2014 (11 years ago); the career has since pivoted entirely to web services, full-stack TypeScript/Node.js, and modern systems programming—not industrial automation C++ development. The candidate also lacks the required engineering degree (B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech), and shows no experience with industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443), message brokers, MQTT protocols, or specific industrial standards (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA), making them unable to contribute meaningfully to this domain on day one.
strengths: Exceptional software craftsmanship with proven mastery of TDD, testing, and code quality—directly aligned with secure development and high standards expectations
Demonstrated ability to architect and optimize high-performance, concurrent systems, including low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby and event-driven platforms at GISCollective
Strong mentoring capability evidenced through organized workshops, teaching roles, and open-source contributions showing ability to elevate team technical practices
concerns: C++ expertise is severely outdated (last hands-on C++ role ended 2015; current 10 years in TypeScript/Node.js), posing significant risk for leading a C++-centric industrial platform team

Educational background does not meet stated requirement (BS Computer Science versus required B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech engineering degree); zero evidence of industrial cybersecurity, IEC 62443 compliance, or domain-specific protocols

Career trajectory is entirely web and modern systems-focused with no exposure to industrial automation, IoT, message brokers, MQTT, or industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA), eliminating ability to contribute domain expertise
